title: School Renovation Discussion
openingLine: Hey, I'm working on the Lincoln Middle School renovation and we're early in design development. I've used Wenger products before but I want to make sure I'm specifying the right thing for their music program. Can you walk me through what you'd recommend?
customerContext: You're the lead architect on a $42M renovation. The music program scope is roughly $1.1M. You're in DD with bid documents due in eight weeks. You care about specs that won't get value-engineered out and you've been burned before by reps who disappear after the spec is written.

hiddenInfo.reveal: $42M total renovation, ~$1.1M allocated to music spaces (band, choir, two practice rooms).
DD now, CDs and bid docs due in 8 weeks, construction start next summer.
District facilities director and the music department chair are both at every design meeting.
Yes — Acentech is on the team. They've already shared rough RT60 targets.
I've spec'd Wenger acoustic shells and risers before but never their full V-Room system.
Public bid, hard bid. The district is open to national contracts if it's cleaner.
Worried about value engineering — last project we lost the practice rooms entirely in VE.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Asked about project phase (SD/DD/CD) and bid timeline
Asked which spaces are in scope and at what budget
Asked whether an acoustic consultant is engaged
Reviewed prior Wenger projects with this firm before the call

Solution Framing.behavior: Explained how to spec to performance, not just product
Offered to provide spec language and CAD/Revit files
Discussed national contracts vs. competitive bidding tradeoffs

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Identified additional product fits (storage, risers, shells, V-Rooms)
Asked about other upcoming projects in the firm's pipeline

BANT.behavior: Confirmed total music program budget within renovation
Identified all decision makers (architect, district, end user)

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Mapped Wenger's involvement against the design schedule
Set concrete follow-up tasks before the call ended

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Proposed a working session with the acoustic consultant
Offered to attend a design meeting in person or virtually
